title: QUIC CRT server-role 1-RTT publication work item
completion_notes: The server-role 1-RTT publication slice now derives and exposes directional open/protect material only after peer Finished verification, keeps the 1-RTT data path closed, and rejects premature, repeated, conflicting, malformed, or terminal publication.
out_of_scope: New trust, identity, or hostname policy work.
summary:
Add the smallest permanent server-role 1-RTT packet-protection publication slice on the existing managed TLS seam.
Keep the bridge-visible open/protect material explicit and server-only.
Do not add 1-RTT data-path processing, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.
verification_plan:
Run the server-role Finished publication tests, the bridge-state rejection tests, the client-role regression test, and the repo-local SpecTrace validation checks.
Run the benchmark that exercises the server Finished publication path if the benchmark artifact is present.
Keep the proof boundary narrow: publication is valid only after server-role `PeerFinishedVerified`.
planned_changes:
- Add explicit server-role 1-RTT open/protect material updates to the managed TLS key schedule.
- Extend the bridge state with directional 1-RTT material storage and deterministic rejection of premature, repeated, conflicting, malformed, or terminal publication.
- Add requirement-home coverage for the publication boundary, rejection paths, and client-role regression.
- Keep the 1-RTT data path, key update, endpoint-host wiring, and interop harness unsupported.
